details.binding_requirement: Launch must go through a carrier that proves the visible Windows Terminal window before mutating runtime binding authority: before-window snapshot, unique new carrier window/id, inhabited child claim, exactly one new visible `CASCADIA_HOSTING_WINDOW_CLASS` HWND, SQLite binding to admitted identity, then OSL projection refresh. Fail closed on missing/mismatched claim or ambiguous/missing window delta. Do not infer identity from terminal title, process order, or foreground focus.
details.materializer: Current local materializer is `tools/operator-surface-carriers/Install-AgentDesktopShortcuts.ps1`. Default output is `.crew/agent-shortcuts`; optional `-DesktopPath` redirects projection to Desktop or another folder.
details.portable_authority: `operator-surfaces/agent-launch-affordances.json` owns portable launch affordance intent. The schema/doc is `operator-surfaces/agent-launch-affordances.schema.md`. Records include `affordance_id`, `label`, admitted `identity_name`, `runtime`, `enabled`, and `materializations` such as `desktop_shortcut`.
details.projection_boundary: Shortcut files under `.crew/agent-shortcuts` or Desktop are projections, not authority. They should be untracked/local materializations.
details.shortcut_shape: Per-agent shortcuts are named like `Narada Agent - Kevin (codex).lnk`; aggregate shortcut is `Narada Agents - All (codex).lnk`.

payload.local_caveat: A prior OSM attempt for this packet was refused by the User Site operator-surface bus because cross-site binding lookup for `narada.architect` is not authority-bearing from `narada-andrey`. This staged inbox path is used as the admitted cross-Site handoff fallback.
suggested_narada_work: Add or standardize `operator-surfaces/agent-launch-affordances.json` for Narada proper identities such as `narada.architect` and `narada.builder`.
Provide an admitted materializer equivalent to `Install-AgentDesktopShortcuts.ps1` that emits `.crew/agent-shortcuts` by default and can target Desktop explicitly.
Keep identity admission and binding authority in agent-context/operator-surface authority stores, not shortcut filenames.
Add smoke coverage that materialization refuses non-admitted identities and launched carrier performs proof-before-bind.
payload.summary: Operator requested direct delivery to Narada. This packet documents the narada-andrey pattern for portable crew/agent shortcut intent, local materialization into `.lnk` files, and proof-before-bind safety requirements.
payload.title: Crew shortcut setup packet from narada-andrey
